title: platanthera unalascensis subsp. maritima
intro: Platanthera unalascensis subsp. maritima, also known as the coastal bog orchid, is a rare and endangered subspecies of orchid native to coastal areas of Alaska. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, reduce fever, and prevent infections. The health benefits of Platanthera unalascensis subsp. maritima include its potential to aid in the treatment of various conditions such as eczema, acne, and respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the roots and flowers in hot water, which can be used as a tea or applied topically as a salve to harness its medicinal properties.
